mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month change in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects aggregate income growth and consumption support, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS changes exhibit a Rank IC below 0.03 relative to 20-day S&P 500 forward returns over any 3-year rolling window, or the top quintile of PAYEMS changes fails to generate a higher mean forward return than the bottom quintile (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5 percent, restrictive monetary policy suppresses aggregate demand and elevates equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5 percent, or the mean forward return during triggers is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales (RSAFS) coinciding with an elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS above 5 percent) creates a double drag on corporate revenues and discount rates, producing sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and FEDFUNDS yields a t-statistic below 2 in predicting 60-day returns over any 5-year rolling window, or the conditional mean return is positive when both RSAFS is declining and FEDFUNDS exceeds 5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, an increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals an impending growth downshift and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances, or the mean forward return during triggers is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) signals improving manufacturing momentum and corporate earnings capacity, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes fail to produce positive 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of months, or the 5-day forward return rank correlation with INDPRO changes is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts (HOUST) above 1.2 million units annualized restores residential investment and upstream commodity demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1200, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of observations, or the mean 60-day return during these triggers does not exceed the unconditional mean return (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) confirms a broad-based expansion spanning services and manufacturing, amplifying earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The bivariate signal where both PAYEMS and INDPRO rise fails to outperform the unconditional market return (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term carries a negative coefficient in a regression of 20-day returns over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A high level of consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) above 310 indicates entrenched inflation that sustains elevated discount rates and margin pressure, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 310, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances, or the mean 5-day return during these triggers is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.
